contributions.detail: Buyer-voiced preference: Astrid says Zycus is favored 'in confidence' (Jul 8), and Jul 9 update confirms budget green-lit at exco level with project moving forward.
Champion (Pam) has direct access and controls the vendor vote; MEDDPICC shows champion, pain, and decision-criteria confirmed.

Economic Buyer engagement is only partial — CPO Rohan is engaged directly, but the CFO (true EB) hasn't met Zycus yet; rep only 'likely' to get 2–3 weeks out. ► Secure the CFO meeting before RFP responses are due.
Coupa remains the dominant active competitor, pushing hard on price ('overpricing' per Pam) and still in the mix after 180+ days. ► Prep a pricing/value defense ahead of Pam's guidance session.
No commercial/paper progress — MEDDPICC paper_process is a gap, and no pricing discussion is documented despite the verbal budget green light. ► Push for a documented commercial proposal.

Exception: Close date (Jul 30) is not credible — RFP only went out this week (Jul 8) with requirements still pending, so the deal is behind its Best Case/Shortlisted positioning. ► Correct the close date/forecast category to match the real RFP timeline.
Pam (Director Procurement, controls the vendor vote) initiated a dinner meeting herself on Jul 9 — buyer-driven, not rep-chased.
CPO/eval team attended ProcureCon Jul 8, and Astrid confirmed Zycus is favored 'in confidence' — real two-way engagement inside the last 14 days.
Continuous cadence since Jan 2026 (RFI, demo, NDA, presentations, multiple F2F touches) with no unexplained gaps over 6 months.
No future meeting is scheduled in CRM (next_scheduled_meeting: null) even though the RFP window just opened. ► Lock the CFO meeting and Pam's RFP-guidance session on the calendar now.
Only 1 meeting logged in Salesforce in the last 30 days despite active July cadence described in Next Step. ► Log the ProcureCon and dinner touches as Tasks/Events so pipeline reflects reality.
Pam self-initiated a dinner meeting and is personally steering internal guidance on how Zycus should respond to the RFP.
CPO/eval team is organizing travel to ProcureCon and Horizon events, and budget has been confirmed at exco level (Jul 9).
No formal procurement/security review or reference-check activity has been documented yet.
No commercial paper is in motion — no proposal accepted, no pricing round documented (paper_process = gap).
No Economic Buyer directly engaged — CFO meeting still pending; Rohan/Pam remain the single points of contact.
Coupa is actively pushing hard on price, and Pam has flagged 'overpricing' concern directly — a live threat, not dormant.
Close date is only 21 days out yet RFP requirements just came out this week with no pricing, paper, or EB alignment — the date is not defensible as recorded.
Funding caution was raised as recently as May 26; the Jul 9 'budget green light' is verbal only, with no signed approval evidenced.
Partial offset: buyer's own team (Astrid, CPO) describes Zycus as favored, which tempers the competitive and EB risk somewhat.
